indication: Treatment of advanced renal cell carcinoma (RCC) in adults following prior vascular endothelial growth factor (VEGF)-targeted therapy

chemotherapy_drugs.special_instructions: Swallow tablets whole — do not crush. Nothing to eat for at least 2 hours before and 1 hour after taking tablets. If dose missed and less than 12 hours until next dose, skip the missed dose. Cabometyx tablets and Cometriq capsules are NOT bioequivalent — do not interchange. Available as 20mg, 40mg and 60mg tablets; dose modifications in multiples of these strengths. 12 cycles set in ARIA; continue until disease progression or intolerance. NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy alert applies.

take_home_medicines.frequency: once daily
take_home_medicines.duration: 28 days per cycle; continue until progression or intolerance
take_home_medicines.notes: Oral chemotherapy. Swallow whole. Fasting 2 hours before and 1 hour after each dose. Available as 20mg, 40mg, 60mg tablets.

dose_modifications.condition: Neutrophils less than 1.5 x10⁹/L or platelets less than 100 x10⁹/L
dose_modifications.notes: Discuss with consultant; low counts may reflect bone marrow infiltration rather than drug toxicity

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 2 elevated ALT, AST or bilirubin for >1 week
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction (1st reduction to 40mg, 2nd reduction to 20mg)

dose_modifications.condition: Grade 3 or above elevated liver enzymes/bilirubin
dose_modifications.notes: Withhold until resolved to baseline, then restart at reduced dose

dose_modifications.parameter: Non-haematological – NCI-CTC grade 3 or intolerable grade 2
dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or greater toxicities or intolerable grade 2 toxicities
dose_modifications.notes: Interrupt treatment; resume at reduced dose (40mg then 20mg) once toxicity improves to grade 1. Third dose reduction: stop treatment.
Stop treatment; re-start at reduced dose once resolved. Initiate loperamide at first sign of diarrhoea.

dose_modifications.condition: Persistent blood pressure >150/100 mmHg despite antihypertensives
dose_modifications.notes: Consider dose reduction; discontinue for severe hypertension despite dose reduction and antihypertensives
Discontinue cabozantinib permanently if PRES diagnosed
Discontinue permanently; hold at least 28 days before invasive dental procedures

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Oral continuous dosing — 28-day cycle. Dose reductions: 1st to 40mg, 2nd to 20mg. Third dose reduction: stop treatment.

ECG at baseline and weeks 2, 4, 8, 12, then every 12 weeks (QTc must not exceed 480 msec)
Blood pressure prior to each cycle; hypertension must be well controlled before starting
Urine dipstick for protein prior to every cycle
Oral examination prior to initiation and periodically during therapy (osteonecrosis of jaw risk)
special_warnings: Cabometyx (tablets) and Cometriq (capsules) are NOT bioequivalent — do not interchange
NPSA/2008/RRR001 oral chemotherapy safety alert applies
Many significant drug interactions — always check before prescribing or dispensing
QT interval prolongation risk — ECG monitoring required; QTc must not exceed 480 msec
Serious and sometimes fatal perforations, fistulas and intra-abdominal abscesses reported
Posterior Reversible Encephalopathy Syndrome (PRES) reported — perform brain MRI for seizures, confusion, altered mental status
Osteonecrosis of the jaw — oral examination required before and during treatment; hold 28 days before invasive dental procedures
Hypertension must be well controlled prior to starting
Monitor for palmer-plantar erythrodysaesthesia — dose interruption may be required
Patients to fast 2 hours before and 1 hour after each dose
Continue until disease progression or intolerance (12 cycles in ARIA)
Toxicity more common in patients ≥75 years

take_home_medicines.notes: Supply 10 days or original pack to cover day 1 and 8

dose_modifications.notes: Delay until recovery then give 75% of original dose
Delay until recovery then re-start at 75%
Initiate at 800mg/m² (local practice, may vary from published sources)

dose_modifications.condition: NCI-CTC grade 3 or above non-haematological toxicity
dose_modifications.notes: Delay until resolved to grade 1 or below; then reduce causative agent to 75% or discontinue

dose_modifications.condition: If gemcitabine dose reduced on day 1
dose_modifications.notes: Continue with reduced dose for day 8

treatment_delay_criteria.notes: Day 1 criteria for both carboplatin and gemcitabine. Day 8 gemcitabine-only criteria apply separately. If third dose reduction required, stop treatment.

monitoring_requirements: FBC, LFTs and U&Es prior to day 1 and day 8 of treatment
Calculated or measured creatinine clearance prior to each cycle
EDTA creatinine clearance may be considered prior to cycle 1 or if significant renal function changes occur during treatment
special_warnings: Palliative intent — 21-day cycle for 6 cycles
Carboplatin dose calculated using Calvert formula (AUC 4.5); requires accurate creatinine clearance
Carboplatin omitted if CrCl <20 ml/min
Gemcitabine starting dose 800mg/m² if bilirubin >30 µmol/L (local practice)
Gemcitabine dose-banded at 38mg/ml per national bands
Radiosensitising potential of gemcitabine — relevant if prior or concurrent radiotherapy
Day 8 gemcitabine modifications independent of day 1; if day 1 dose reduced, continue with reduced dose on day 8

auc_dosing.gfr_method: Calculated or measured creatinine clearance (EDTA for cycle 1 or if significant changes)

required_patient_fields.weight: Required for Calvert formula and BSA-based gemcitabine dosing
required_patient_fields.creatinine_clearance: Required prior to each cycle for Calvert formula; EDTA recommended cycle 1

drugs.special_instructions: Dose calculated using Calvert formula. Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml). Omit if CrCl <20 ml/min. No hepatic dose adjustment required. Extravasation risk: irritant.
drugs.notes: per cycle | Dose calculated using Calvert formula. Dose banded per national dose bands (10mg/ml). Omit if CrCl <20 ml/min. No hepatic dose adjustment required. Extravasation risk: irritant.

drugs.special_instructions: Dose banded per national dose bands (38mg/ml). If bilirubin >30 µmol/L initiate at 800mg/m². If CrCl <30 ml/min consider dose reduction. Extravasation risk: neutral.
drugs.notes: per cycle | Dose banded per national dose bands (38mg/ml). If bilirubin >30 µmol/L initiate at 800mg/m². If CrCl <30 ml/min consider dose reduction. Extravasation risk: neutral.
